ruleset.name: Basic Rules (JavaTddPlugin version)
ruleset.description: The Basic Ruleset contains a collection of good practices which everyone should follow. This version of the ruleset includes alternate messages from the main PMD rulesets/java/basic.xml ruleset.

rule.message: Check the update part of the inner for loop. It appears that you are incrementing the wrong loop variable. It is either a mistake, or it will be confusing to others.
If a for loop does not have an initialization expression or an update expression, you should use a while loop instead.
Do not place return statements in finally blocks, since that may silently discard meaningful exceptions.
The condition of this if statement is always true or always false, so you do not need the if statement at all.
These two if statements should be combined into one using &&.

This literal number starts with a zero, indicating it is written in octal (base 8). Avoid octal literals, because they are easily confused with decimal (base 10). To improve readability, replace this with a decimal value instead.
Using multiple unary operators may be a bug, and is confusing. To improve readability, simplify your expression or split it into multiple statements (with comments explaining what is going on).
Avoid using a branching statement as the last in a loop. This is confusing, and may be a bug. Ensure that it is not a bug, and then restructure your loop's logic to avoid this situation.
